summary: Frontend-only e2e UI testing of PJ Ours cafe SPA (React, no backend). Exercised all 17 requested flows via Playwright with window.open monkey-patched. All critical flows pass: landing render, cart drawer, menu rendering (175 items across 11 categories), search filter, add/increment/decrement/remove, parcel calculation (₹5 × qty), all 4 validation cases (empty form, missing outlet, delivery without address), WhatsApp URL construction for both outlets (East Fort 919590012678, West Fort 917012611090) with properly URL-encoded order message including outlet name and grand total, floating cart visibility, and mobile viewport. Zero console/page errors observed.
critical_code_review_comments: CartDrawer.jsx (310 lines) and MenuPage.jsx (183 lines) are within healthy size, no split needed.
Phone validation regex `/^[+0-9\s-]{7,}$/` is lenient (accepts non-Indian numbers); acceptable for MVP but consider stricter E.164 if needed.
Cart state lives only in React context (no localStorage persistence) — a full page reload will wipe the cart. Consider persisting cart to localStorage for better UX.
buildOrderMessage uses CR-less `\n` joining which wa.me handles fine; verified decoded message contains Outlet, Name, Phone, Order Type, itemized list, Subtotal, Parcel, Total.
Outlet radio is required and correctly blocks WhatsApp open until selected — matches PRD constraint.
Parcel formula ₹5 × itemCount verified: 3 total qty → ₹15; grand total = subtotal + parcel (verified ₹170 + ₹15 = ₹185).
window.open uses `noopener` target which is good practice.
No data-testid missing on any interactive element exercised; all selectors resolved.
test_credentials: N/A - no auth in this app
context_for_next_testing_agent: App is a pure React SPA using REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL only for preview host. All 175 menu items render under 11 CATEGORIES. Data-testids in place on interactive elements: header-cart-button, header-cart-count, cart-drawer, cart-items-list, cart-item-<id>, cart-increment-<id>, cart-decrement-<id>, cart-remove-<id>, cart-qty-<id>, cart-parcel-row, cart-grand-total, input-customer-name, input-customer-phone, input-customer-address, input-customer-note, order-type-pickup/delivery, outlet-option-eastfort/westfort, place-order-whatsapp, floating-cart-button, menu-search, cat-tab-<slug>, menu-add-<id>, menu-item-<id>. When testing 'Place Order via WhatsApp' monkey-patch window.open before click to capture the URL (drawer closes on success). The drawer is a scrollable Sheet — always use locator.scroll_into_view_if_needed() before clicking drawer-inner controls at 1920x1080 desktop viewport.
